senses.definition: a type of card game in which players compete to win tricks, which are groups of cards played in a single round according to the rules of the game

examples.x: In a trick-taking game like Bridge, good communication and strategy are key to winning.
She was excited to teach her friends how to play a trick-taking game she had learned on vacation.
The game of Pinochle is a classic trick-taking game with complex rules and scoring.
Trick-taking games like Euchre are popular at family gatherings and friendly competitions.

senses.definition: a card game for 2-6 players in which they try to gather points by collecting sets of cards, using 2 decks of cards and 4 jokers

examples.x: One needs two decks of cards to play Canasta with friends.
Learning the rules of Canasta can be challenging but is very enjoyable.
Canasta became popular because it is both competitive and fun.
The players had to agree on the final score needed to win the Canasta match.
Using jokers and wild cards strategically is key in Canasta.

senses.definition: a card game, usually for two players, who try to score a specific point before the other player and during the game record their points by placing small wooden pieces called pegs into the holes of a board

examples.x: Cribbage is a fun game to play on quiet evenings with friends.
The wooden board used to keep score is one of the unique features of cribbage.
Learning cribbage can take some time, but it becomes easier with practice.
Many people enjoy cribbage for its mix of strategy and luck.
Playing cribbage is a great way to sharpen mental math skills.

senses.definition: a game of cards in which players must gather 10 points in order to win

examples.x: Gin rummy is a fun and competitive card game that is easy to pick up.
They spent the evening playing gin rummy with a few friends.
They decided to play gin rummy while waiting for dinner to be ready.
She won the last round of gin rummy by forming a perfect sequence.

senses.definition: a card game in which two or more players bet on the values of the cards handed to them, often played in order to win money
a game of cards that its players aim to form the right combinations of sequences or sets
a card game in which players place their cards down one by one and the winner is someone who first says 'snap' as soon as noticing two similar cards are put together
a card game that has only one player
a card game that is typically played with a standard deck of 52 cards by three or more players, with the objective of getting rid of all of one's cards by playing them face down in a pile

a simple card game that is typically played by two players with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to win all of the cards in the deck by playing higher-ranking cards than one's opponent
a classic card game that is typically played by two to four players with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to get rid of all of one's cards by playing them onto a discard pile

examples.x: We spent the afternoon playing Crazy Eights and laughing with friends.

After losing several rounds, I finally managed to win in Crazy Eights.
We played Crazy Eights until late at night, trying to beat each other.

senses.definition: a fast-paced card game that is typically played by two or more players with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to win all of the cards in the deck by slapping the pile when certain combinations of cards are played

examples.x: We played Egyptian Ratscrew for hours last night, and I ended up winning all the cards.
He was really fast at slapping the pile in Egyptian Ratscrew, making it tough for anyone else to win.
My friends taught me how to play Egyptian Ratscrew, and it turned out to be a lot of fun.
We couldn’t stop laughing during our Egyptian Ratscrew match, especially when someone accidentally slapped a wrong pile.
Egyptian Ratscrew is one of those games where the more you play, the faster your reflexes get.

senses.definition: a simple card game that is typically played by two to six players with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to collect sets of four cards of the same rank by asking other players for cards of that rank
a single-player card game that is played with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to move all of the cards to four foundation piles in ascending order, separated by suit

examples.x: I spent hours playing FreeCell on my phone to pass the time.
In FreeCell, you need to carefully plan each move to avoid getting stuck.
The game of FreeCell is won when all the cards are moved to the foundation piles.
I find FreeCell to be a great way to improve my concentration and problem-solving skills.
He spent the afternoon playing FreeCell, trying to solve every puzzle without any mistakes.

senses.definition: a solitaire card game that is played with two decks of standard playing cards, with the objective of removing all of the cards from the tableau by creating sequences of cards in descending order, regardless of suit
a popular single-player card game that is played with a standard deck of 52 cards

a fast-paced card game that is typically played by two players with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to get rid of all of one's cards by playing them onto a central pile as quickly as possible

a fast-paced card game that is typically played by two or more players with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to win all of the cards by being the first player to slap a jack when it is played

examples.x: Slapjack is a great way to test your reflexes while having fun with friends.

senses.definition: a card game where players aim to be the first to get rid of all their cards by playing them in specific sequences or following certain rules

examples.x: Crazy Eights is a popular shedding-type card game where the goal is to get rid of all your cards.
In a shedding-type card game, players must carefully plan their moves to avoid getting stuck with too many cards.
Shedding-type card games are usually fast-paced, with players racing to empty their hands before their opponents.
A good strategy in a shedding-type card game is to pay attention to the cards your opponents are playing.
The excitement in a shedding-type card game increases when only a few cards remain in the players’ hands.

senses.definition: a simple card game that is typically played with a standard deck of 52 cards by two or more players, in which the objective is to avoid being left with the unpaired "Old Maid" card at the end of the game
a popular Austrian card game played with a 20-card deck, where players aim to win tricks and accumulate points through various card combinations

examples.x: We played Herzeln with our friends last night, and it was a great way to spend time together.

The game of Herzeln uses a 20-card deck, which makes it different from many other card games.
We played a few rounds of Herzeln, and I managed to win by keeping my points low.

senses.definition: a card game played with a special deck where players aim to be the first to discard all their cards by matching them with the top card of the pile

examples.x: My sister is unbeatable at Uno; she always seems to know the right move.
I always get excited when we play Uno, especially when I have a good hand.

senses.definition: a type of game where players play cards in a continuous, fast-paced manner without taking turns, and the gameplay occurs in real-time, requiring quick thinking and reaction to the changing game state

examples.x: In a real-time card game, players must act quickly and think fast to keep up with the action.
Unlike traditional card games, a real-time card game requires all players to play at the same time.
The excitement of real-time card games comes from the fast-paced nature and constant decisions players must make.
A real-time card game like Spit can get very intense as players try to outpace each other.
Playing a real-time card game often leads to moments of chaos, where players must stay focused to win.

senses.definition: a shedding-type card game where players aim to be the first to get rid of all their cards by playing higher-ranked cards or specific card combinations
a card game that involves deductive reasoning and is played with a standard deck of cards, where players try to figure out a secret rule or pattern governing the play of cards

a two-player card game played with multiple decks of cards, where players aim to be the first to play all their cards from their individual stockpiles to build ascending piles in the center while blocking their opponent's moves

examples.x: She quickly figured out the best way to win at Spite and Malice by blocking her opponent’s moves.

The game of Spite and Malice involves a lot of careful planning and observation.
He was so focused on winning that he didn’t realize he had given me the chance to play my last card in Spite and Malice.

senses.definition: a card game played with a standard deck of cards, where players aim to have the lowest score by forming specific card combinations and attempting to get rid of high-value cards

a unique card game with unconventional rules and secretive nature, where players must deduce and learn the rules as they play, often resulting in unexpected penalties for breaking unknown rules or making mistakes during the game

an Italian card game played with a standard deck of cards, where players aim to capture specific cards on the table by matching their values with cards from their hand and earn points for combinations of cards captured
a fast-paced card game where players try to collect four-of-a-kind cards and quickly grab a spoon from the center of the table, leading to a fun and competitive race
a strategic card game typically played with a standard deck of cards, where players aim to form specific card combinations and avoid exceeding a certain total point value in their hand

senses.definition: a two-player solitaire card game played with a standard deck of cards, where players try to move their cards onto common foundation piles by building up in suit and sequence

senses.definition: a card game played with a standard deck of cards where players aim to be the first to get rid of their cards by playing higher-ranked cards or specific card combinations
a card game played with a standard deck of cards by two to four players, where players capture cards on the table by matching cards from their hand with the same value

senses.definition: a traditional Japanese card game that involves players listening to poetry readings and quickly identifying and capturing matching cards based on the poems' texts or first lines
a traditional Japanese card game played with a deck of hanafuda cards, involving matching cards based on their pictures and suits to form specific combinations

examples.x: I learned how to play Hanafuda when I visited Japan last summer.

senses.definition: a type of card game that includes multiple games played with a single deck of cards, offering a variety of different gameplay experiences within one set

examples.x: He brought out a compendium card game during the road trip, so everyone could play something different.

senses.definition: a card game that is typically played with a standard deck of 52 cards by two or more players, in which the objective is to get rid of all of one's cards by playing them face down, while bluffing about the cards being played
